Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A Decade of Slug 701 upvotes, 68 comments
  2. More than 135 open hardware devices flashable with your own firmware 310 upvotes, 47 comments
  3. Write up of my homebrew CPU build 188 upvotes, 27 comments
  4. A tiny, decentralised tool to explore the small web 44 upvotes, 4 comments
  5. Mistral AI Releases Forge 659 upvotes, 166 comments
  6. Nightingale – open-source karaoke app that works with any song on your computer 366 upvotes, 91 comments
  7. Show HN: Sub-millisecond VM sandboxes using CoW memory forking 251 upvotes, 62 comments
  8. JPEG Compression 376 upvotes, 102 comments
  9. On a Boat 94 upvotes, 20 comments
  10. Using calculus to do number theory 47 upvotes, 9 comments
  11. Pandas Exercises for Data Analysis (Interactive) 114 upvotes, 30 comments
  12. Celebrating Tony Hoare's mark on computer science 93 upvotes, 26 comments
  13. The pleasures of poor product design 209 upvotes, 71 comments
  14. Snowflake AI Escapes Sandbox and Executes Malware 49 upvotes, 12 comments
  15. Federal Cyber Experts Called Microsoft's Cloud "A Pile of Shit", yet Approved It 207 upvotes, 88 comments
  16. A ngrok-style secure tunnel server written in Rust and Open Source 34 upvotes, 10 comments
  17. OpenRocket 24 upvotes, 6 comments
  18. Nvidia NemoClaw 37 upvotes, 12 comments
  19. Machine Payments Protocol (MPP) 54 upvotes, 20 comments
  20. Rob Pike's Rules of Programming (1989) 562 upvotes, 308 comments
  21. Meta will shut down VR Horizon Worlds access June 15 49 upvotes, 19 comments
  22. Death to Scroll Fade 114 upvotes, 55 comments
  23. A data center opened next door. Then came the high-pitched whine 25 upvotes, 8 comments
  24. Wander – A tiny, decentralised tool (just 2 files) to explore the small web 20 upvotes, 6 comments
  25. A Tiny Camera Revealed a Hidden Passage in the Great Pyramid 78 upvotes, 40 comments
  26. Restoring the first recording of computer music (2018) 16 upvotes, 5 comments
  27. A Fuzzer for the Toy Optimizer 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  28. Wide logging: Stripe's canonical log line pattern 6 upvotes, 1 comments
  29. North Korean's 100k fake IT workers net $500M a year for Kim 18 upvotes, 13 comments
  30. Ndea (YC W26) is hiring a symbolic RL search guidance lead 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 1:00am, 19 March 2026. Source here.
